signals.title: USD/GBP — Hawkish Fed + G10 Strength + GBP Weakness
signals.text: USD 1W avg +1.005% (n=7 pairs) vs GBP 1W avg -0.713% (n=4 pairs) — a 1.718% leader-to-laggard spread. The dollar index (DXY) rallied to a 13-month high (+0.63% 1D) following the Fed's hawkish policy update, signaling possible higher interest rates. GBP/USD at 1.3222 slides as the hawkish Fed overshadows the BoE's hold. The 170bp USD carry premium (SOFR 3.63% vs ESTR 1.93%) further supports USD strength against the EUR, while USD/GBP carry is neutral (0bp). Watch for further Fed commentary to confirm sustained USD bid.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Intervention Risk + Carry + COT
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.4 — testing the 160.00 intervention watch level. The 290bp Fed-BoJ carry (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%) sustains the bid, despite JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (crowded carry trade). BoJ is hiking (1.0% policy rate) with a positive OIS bias, creating a significant COT vs OIS divergence. Intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00, potentially triggering a short-squeeze. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 50% hike priced) determines the yield gap direction.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,354 (specs fading a hawkish CB) vs RBNZ hike bias. This is a clear COT-OIS divergence. Retail sentiment shows 93% long, an extreme contrarian signal against the institutional short. If RBNZ delivers or signals a hike at the next meeting, short-squeeze risk is elevated, targeting 0.5900+. NZD/USD at 0.5761. Watch the next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 Jul for catalyst.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Elevated Vol Premium + Negative CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD IV 14.1% vs HV30 7.9% — a +6.2% VRP, indicating elevated options market hedging demand above realized volatility. This is compounded by a moderate negative CESI (net -8 misses), suggesting data is consistently disappointing expectations. AUD/USD at 0.7025. The elevated VRP suggests caution despite the +72bp AUD carry premium (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.63%). Watch for a shift in economic data surprises.
